entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello,

I am excited to express my interest in participating in the Digital Skills Initiative, with a particular focus on Supply Chain Management (SCM) in SAP. I am a recent graduate with a strong background in Supply Chain Management, specializing in Procurement. During my academic journey, I familiarized myself with SAP through an introductory course on SAP ERP, and I have also completed courses in SAP S/4HANA Sales and Distribution, as well as Material Management.

My goal is to enhance my expertise in SAP to streamline procurement and logistics operations, as I believe incorporating this technology will allow me to make significant contributions in my field. By participating in this initiative, I hope to acquire advanced digital skills and achieve a certification in SCM, which will help me extend my career path and become a proficient professional in this area.

Thank you!

entry.title: Mentors wanted: Spark Interest in STEM – Make it a Girl Thing!
entry.content:
Dear SAP Women in Tech Community members,

We’re very excited to share with you, that Germany's largest online mentoring program for girls in STEM, CyberMentor, is starting its next round. A lot of girls, the future mentees, have already signed up and are now looking for you to become their mentor to discover STEM activities and dive deeper into the world of STEM professions.

What's it all about?

At CyberMentor, up to 800 female students are mentored for the duration of one year by female professionals working in STEM fields. As a role model, the mentor encourages STEM activities and provides advice on study and career choices. Mentoring takes place via a protected online platform with mail, chat and forum and is supported by a wide range of information about the STEM world.

What is expected of you as a mentor?

  • You're a women in STEM.
  • You’re located in Germany for the mentoring period (one year).
  • You speak either German or English, or both.
  • You will mentor a 5th to 13th grade student online for one year.
  • You will spend 30 minutes each week with your mentee.
  • You support your mentee in STEM projects, discuss STEM topics and talk about your studies and/or your profession.

As a mentor, you become part of a nationwide network of women in STEM. You will receive an introduction and training to support you in your role as a mentor.

How can you participate?

Participating is easy: Register on CyberMentor.de by September 25th.

You want to participate in an Info-call before registering? Then check out the list of Info-Calls offered here.

 

Want to share more with us?

Are there any similar online mentoring programs in your country like CyberMentor in Germany? Leave a comment and let us know!

entry.title: How to restrict both PO line items and additional G/L account amounts in MIRO?
entry.content:
Hi Team,

During invoicing with MIRO or app Create Supplier Invoice, if user selected PO line items and additional amount in GL system allowed to post entries. How we can restrict it?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to restrict both PO line items and additional G/L...
entry.content:
In standard, MIRO accepts both kinds of items. From software-design perspective, there is no error.

MIRO postings can be restricted based on a custom logic via BAdI MRM_HEADER_CHECK.

You can refer to SAP Note 1156325 - BAdIs in the Logistics Invoice Verification environment for details.

entry.title: Re: CMD_API 100 --> Customer specific data were not saved...
entry.content:
Here are two ways to tackle it:

OBA5 Method:

Go to Transaction OBA5. Set the Application Area to F2. You can either customize the message settings as needed or simply switch off the message.

OBMSG Method:

Go to Transaction OBMSG. Select F2 and look for message type 141. You’ll see an option to "Switch Off" the message to stop the error from showing.

entry.title: Unas cuantas actualizaciones de SAP Business One Support
entry.content:
Recientemente, hemos entregado una sesión sobre HANA y actualmente estamos ocupados preparando una sesión en el área de conocimiento del Integration Framework. Más sobre eso a continuación. 

Como esta es una nueva iniciativa donde estamos tratando de encontrar una forma diferente de compartir el conocimiento y las perspectivas únicas que tenemos como organización de soporte, realmente no sabíamos cómo sería recibida. La recepción ha sido buena. Después de 2 sesiones, hemos tenido más de 500 asistentes. También hemos recibido muchos comentarios positivos, tanto en las sesiones como por correo electrónico y en LinkedIn. Gracias por eso.   

En ese sentido, te animamos a que hagas oír tu voz; si tienes comentarios, positivos o negativos, no dudes en usar el campo de comentarios a continuación. Si tienes sugerencias para temas adicionales o preguntas sobre el material presentado, nuevamente, utiliza la sección de comentarios a continuación. Estamos escuchando. 

Próxima sesión: SAP Business One Integration Framework – Administración, Mejores Prácticas y diseño de escenarios. 

En la sesión, nuestro líder del área de producto Pavol Hrasko y dos ingenieros senior cubrirán algunas técnicas de resolución de problemas recomendadas para los puntos críticos más comunes, diseños de escenarios recomendados y tareas administrativas elementales para mejorar la fiabilidad del sistema. 

La sesión tendrá lugar el 24 de septiembre, de 14:00 a 15:00 CEST. Como quizás sepas, la sesión es para socios y es gratuita: 

 

Webinar: SAP Business One Integration Framework

 

Acceso a las sesiones grabadas 

 

Si no pudiste asistir o deseas acceder a las sesiones grabadas que se entregaron anteriormente, consulta a continuación. También tendrás acceso al material que se presentó. Ten en cuenta que aunque se hablan diferentes idiomas, el material siempre está en inglés. 

 

Sesión HANA: 

SAP Business One versión para SAP HANA - Mejores Prácticas en Administración de Bases de Datos (Inglés)

SAP Business One versión para SAP HANA - Mejores Prácticas en Administración de Bases de Datos (Español)

SAP Business One versión para SAP HANA - Mejores Prácticas en Administración de Bases de Datos (Portugués)

 

Sesión de Soporte: 

 

Maximizando tu Experiencia de Soporte con SAP Business One como Socio (Inglés)

Maximizando tu Experiencia de Soporte con SAP Business One como Socio (Chino)

Maximizando tu Experiencia de Soporte con SAP Business One como Socio (Español)

Maximizando tu Experiencia de Soporte con SAP Business One como Socio (Portugués)

 

¿Qué más?

 

Un breve recordatorio sobre nuestras Respuestas Guiadas. El soporte de SAP Business One ha lanzado hasta hoy 28 Respuestas Guiadas diferentes. Encuentra todas aquí: Respuestas Guiadas

 

La comunidad – estamos ahí, utiliza el conocimiento de los ingenieros de soporte y tus compañeros a tu favor.

Los Blogs Spotlight del Soporte – encuéntralos aquí: Consejos del Soporte de SAP Business One

 

Eso es todo por ahora. Esperamos verte en la próxima sesión.

entry.title: Re: Application Job parameters do not refresh
entry.content:
Hello together,

there is an easier workaround without deleting and recreating:

In your Application Job Catalog Entry delete the check class, activate, then paste it again and activate. Then everything gets refreshed.

And yes it took me hours to find it...

Best Regards,
Asif

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

entry.title: Re: Activate Integration & Exception in Cloud ALM for SAP...
entry.content:
According to the SAP Help Page of SAP Datasphere, currently there is no direct connection option available for Cloud ALM. This however, doesn't necessarily mean that it is not possible to connect Cloud ALM to SAP Datasphere. You need to review what connection options exist at Cloud ALM side and verify if they can be utilized to connect to Datasphere. As per Overview | SAP Cloud ALM Analytics | SAP Business Accelerator Hub OData may be used.
 
Please review Preparing Connectivity for Connections | SAP Help Portal for an understanding of the standard connection options available in SAP Datasphere. You may also visit Getting Started with SAP Datasphere | SAP Help Portal and Getting Started | SAP Help Portal for more information.

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud for planning - Filter Data Action based on Dimension Property via Scripting
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

While researching this topic, I came across several discussions where people were asking if it's possible to filter a Data Action based on Dimension Properties. The common answer was "No."

However, I have an idea:

  1. I can create a script that initializes a dropdown menu populated with the Dimension Properties.
  2. The user selects an option from the dropdown.
  3. Based on the user's selection, the script assigns the corresponding Dimension members to a variable.

This variable would then be used to populate the parameter in the Data Action, triggering its execution.

Would this approach work? If so, could you provide some example scripts to help implement this?

 

Kind regards,

ismail

@N1kh1l special thanks to you in advance.

entry.title: Real Talk with Suna Singh | Track Lead for Digital Transformation with Cloud ERP
entry.content:
Hello, SAP Community members 👋

I am Suna Singh, an Operations Expert at Cloud ERP and one of my passions is with SAP TechEd. Beyond that, I like travelling around the world to meet new people and cultures and embrace new adventures. I also love skiing and reading.   

In the track “Digital Transformation with Cloud ERP” you will explore how SAP S/4HANA Cloud can enhance your digital transformation by supporting your business needs from mission-critical operations to business-model innovation. Whether adopting your first ERP or migrating from an existing one to the cloud, you can gain a complete, modular solution with the AI and analytics capabilities through exploring our GROW with SAP and RISE with SAP solutions. Learn ways to extend and embed SAP S/4HANA Cloud and use the power of AI and automation.

What inspired you to specialize in this track? 

To support your digital transformation, we provide a broad range of topics such as transitioning paths to SAP S/4HANA Cloud including tools and services, extensibility, integration with SAP Business Technology Platform, architecture, clean core, AI, user experience, security, and many more. 

How does this track embody the ‘where ideas get real’ spirit? 

Our experts will not only talk about the latest innovations, they will also showcase various demos and share best practices with you.  

In the Jump-Start sessions - DT180 Integrating SAP S/4HANA Cloud with SAP IBP using SAP Integration Suite and DT280 Build clean core extensions with ABAP Cloud for SAP S/4HANA Cloud - you will get the opportunity to gain hands-on experience and complete the exercises at your own pace.  

Track stars (top sessions to keep on your radar) 

  • DT100 | Unlocking the intelligent enterprise: Cloud ERP innovations for the future: Explore the future of cloud ERP, focusing on the latest innovations and strategies driving digital transformation. 
  • DT101 | Choosing the right path for your business to SAP S/4HANA Cloud: Find out about the important factors, explore the right option for your organization, and get insights into SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2408. 
  • DT102 | Tools and services to make your transition to SAP S/4HANA Cloud easier: Discover tools and strategies for your transition to SAP S/4HANA Cloud with the RISE with SAP solution. 
  • DT201 | Extensibility for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: Learn about key user and development tools in the ABAP Cloud development model and how customers and partners can manage their on-stack extensions to SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition.  
  • DT103 | SAP Business AI in cloud ERP: Understand how your business processes are supported by cloud ERP solutions from SAP with the product portfolio enhanced with AI and other intelligent technologies, including traditional machine learning, large language models, generative AI, and situation handling.  

What should be in everyone’s TechEd toolkit for 2024? 

  • Agenda Builder: Build your own agenda in order not to miss any session you are interested in. 
  • Explore: Explore our session offering, learn from industry leaders, and discover best practices from SAP experts and partners. 
  • Inspiration: Join us for a high-energy, interactive virtual event experience and get inspired. 

Want to learn more about the track Digital Transformation with Cloud ERP? Explore the  TechEd 2024 session catalog and ensure your favorite track sessions are on your list.  

Don’t miss the event and and sign up today!

entry.title: First release of B2B Integration Factory – Integration Packages and their Extensions
entry.content:
Introduction

The previous article B2B Integration Factory – B2B related Integration Packages with your Custom Extensions” describes how to obtain your additional requirements for doing B2B integration in a timely manner if they are specifically not supported by the standard product. This will be realized through the extended integration packages of the B2B Integration Factory, which are available at no additional costs in the SAP Business Accelerator Hub’s Community Section. Now that the first releases are available, this article looks at which integration packages were delivered by the B2B Integration Factory and which enhancements are included.

 

Extensions distributed over Integration Packages

To ensure that the enhancements can be implemented logically and consistently, we have created 7 logically separate integration packages, which are introduced in the subchapter Integration Packages. As you can see from the list below, the main integration package is "B2B Integration Factory - Cloud Integration - Trading Partner Management". If you need a further extension to at least one of the integration flows in this integration package, we kindly ask you to submit a feature request via GitHub instead for doing your own local customizations. Any local changes could lead to inconsistencies and problems with newer releases of this integration package.

The integration flows in the other integration packages are predefined integration flows or examples. Users can always create their own integration flows in the respective integration packages. All these integration flows are connected via ProcessDirect to the corresponding main integration flows in the B2B Integration Factory - Cloud Integration - Trading Partner Management integration package. In the "Extensions of First Release" subchapter, you will then find the enhancements that are available in the first release and for what this extension is good for.

Integration Packages in Detail

To manage the various enhancements as efficiently as possible in a consistent way, there are several logical integration packages:

B2B Integration Factory - Cloud Integration - Trading Partner Management

This is the TPM related main processing integration package. It is like the Cloud Integration - Trading Partner Management V2. It covers the following:

  1. Extensions, incorporated by the B2B Integration Factory in the integration flows:
    • Step 1b - Sender Interchange Extraction Flow
    • Step 2 - Interchange Processing Flow
    • Step 3 - Receiver Communication Flow
  2. Another integration flow that supports the custom “Base-Overlay” approach.

B2B Integration Factory - Communication Sender Flows

This integration package provides all integration flows for doing a sender communication and handing over to the “Step 1b - Sender Interchange Extraction Flow”, via a ProcessDirect flow step. Each so-called sender communication flow covers a specific communication protocol such as AS2, supported by its specific sender communication adapter (e.g., AS2 Sender Adapter). Further examples of communication sender flows for the communication protocols POP3/IMAP (Email) and SFTP.

The user can add further sender communication flows into this package. For this purpose, a template “Step 1a – TEMPLATE Sender” is available for copy.

B2B Integration Factory - Communication Receiver Flows (Custom)

This integration package should provide all custom-made communication receiver flows that are not directly supported by the “Step 3 - Receiver Communication Flow”. These additional communication receiver flows will be connected via a ProcessDirect flow step. Each integration flow covers an additional communication protocol that is not supported yet such as SFTP and SMP (Email).

The user can add further receiver communication flows into this package. For this purpose, a template “Step 3b - TEMPLATE Receiver” is available for copy.

B2B Integration Factory - Interchange Extraction Flows

This integration package provides all integration flows for extracting the relevant parameters and key fields from the headers depending on the supported type systems. It especially relates to the XML based type systems. Some extraction flows for XML based SAP IDoc, SAP SOAP, and GS1XML are already provided, which can be locally adjusted. But there is also the integration flow template “TEMPLATE Interchange Extraction Flow” available for copy, which gives you the possibility to create your own extraction flow. It also includes the comprehensive "Extraction Value Mapping" entries, which allows you to substitute a compound of several header parameters in one entry. How to use it is explained in the provided guideline.

B2B Integration Factory - Interchange Assembly Flows

This integration package includes all integration flows, which are responsible for the final assembly of target (receiver) interchange/message payloads according to the conventions of the considered type system.  It is responsible for inserting the relevant data from the TPM accordingly. This integration package already has assembly flows for the type systems: ASC X12, GS1XML, SAP IDoc, SAP SOAP, TRADACOMS and UN/EDIFACT. These can be also changed or extended, if required. But you’ll also find an assembly flow template for the creation of your own assembly flows.

B2B Integration Factory - Extended Interchange Processing Flows

This integration package provides you with some integration flows for modifying or updating the payload content so that these can be finally processed. An example is the modification and enrichment of the  UN/EDIFACT related functional acknowledgement or substituting some of the values in UN/EDIFACT sender interchange so that this can be sufficiently processed.

B2B Integration Factory - Pre- and Post-Processing Flows (Custom)

This integration process is a placeholder for the pre- and post-processing flows which you want to assign in the TPA à Business Transaction Activity à Sender or Reveiver Interchange as customized pre- or post-processing. The package consists of example integration flows which show how sorts or enrichments can be implemented. You’ll also find an integration flow template for the creation of your own pre- and post-processing integration flow.

 

Logical co-operation of the Integration Packages

The following figure shows how these mentioned integration packages work together.

  • B2B Integration Factory - Communication Sender Flows: Starting at the sender side, there is the integration package “B2B Integration Factory - Communication Sender Flows” with all the sender communication flows such as for AS2 or SOAP sender connection.
  • Step 1b - Sender Interchange Extraction Flow: All the received data from the sender independent of the communication protocol will be handed over to the integration flow “Step 1b - Sender Interchange Extraction Flow” which is in the integration package “B2B Integration Factory - Cloud Integration - Trading Partner Management”. Depending on the identified type system and syntax type, this Step 1bflow might call a type system related extraction flow, which is located in the integration package “B2B Integration Factory - Interchange Extraction Flows”.
  • B2B Integration Factory - Interchange Extraction Flows: There are number of type system related extraction flows, which are responsible for extracting a specific set of values and write these into the relevant exchange header parameters. These parameters are for example the sender id, sender id qualifier, receiver id, receiver id qualifier, message type, version, etc. These values are required for finding the appropriate processing content in the Partner Directory. Each type system has their own header structure, and these mentioned data are located differently. Therefore, different rule sets are required.
  • Step 2 - Interchange Processing Flow: This mentioned finding of the processing content in the Partner Directory will happen in the integration flow “Step 2 - Interchange Processing Flow”, which is also responsible for the whole processing of the interchange payload so that it fits to receiver’s requirements. Depending on the requirements this integration flow might call custom flows that are in the integration packages “B2B Integration Factory - Interchange Extraction Flows” and “B2B Integration Factory - Pre- and Post-Processing Flows (Custom)”. Furthermore, it might call the custom based integration flow “Step 2b - Interchange Processing Flow - Base-Overlay Mapping Process“, if a base overlay-based mapping process is required. How this works, will be explained in another blog soon.
  • B2B Integration Factory - Interchange Assembly Flows: Once the message payload is correctly mapped and processed into the appropriate target message type based on the required target, it is necessary to fill the type specific header of this payload with the header specific data that are configured in the Trading Partner Agreement (TPA). Based on the supported type system, there is an individual assembly process flow located in the integration package: “B2B Integration Factory - Interchange Assembly Flows”.
  • Step 3 - Receiver Communication Flow: The finally assembled receiver interchange payload will be handed over to the interchange flow “Step 3 - Receiver Communication Flow” which is responsible to send this payload to the final target system via either one of the supported communication protocols or via the additionally supported communication protocols.
  • B2B Integration Factory - Communication Receiver Flows (Custom): Especially the communication receiver flows of the non-supported communication flows are in the integration package “B2B Integration Factory - Communication Receiver Flows (Custom)”. You will find some examples in here for example EMail.receiver and SFTP.receiver. There is also a template available, which can be used for the creation of some further own communication receiver flows.

 

Released Extensions

The following table shows you the list of extensions in this release. You’ll find further details in the documentation that is provided with the Integration Packages in the Business Accelerator Hub’s Community section.

Extension

Current Standard Implementation

What is possible by this extension?

Syntax Type and Type System related Routing

Just EDI (UN/EDIFACT, ASC X12, TRADACOMS) syntax representation in sender message payloads is supported via TPM.

It allows you to dynamically add further syntax representations such as JSON, CSV, etc. so that these can be supported at sender side, too.

Split of Sender Payloads with Several EDI Interchanges

The TPM system just work with a single interchange (UN/EDIFACT UNB … UNZ, or ASC X12 ISA … IEA) per sender message payload.

It allows you to split into several interchanges, if there are several in the sender message payload.

Modification of EDI extracted key values

Just the extracted values from the supported extraction rules will be used for calculating the PID. This might lead to mismatches.

The possibilities of this extraction value mapping are so extensive that the details of the extraction value mapping are described in a separate blog.. 

Selection of XML based Type Systems

Just the XML type systems: SAP SOAP, SAP IDOC, and GS1XML are supported at sender side.

It allows you to add further XML type systems at sender side.

Extraction of key fields of XML or Flat based Sender Interchange Payload

Just extracts predefined key fields for TPM supported type systems only.

It allows you to define your own rules for extracting key fields, and this is also possible for additional, non-supported type systems.

Further processing of received functional acknowledgement interchanges

It is not possible to further process received functional acknowledgements so that e.g a mapped SAP IDOC SYSTAT message with the status from the functional acknowledgement will be submitted to the S/4HANA OnPremise system.

It allows to enable a further processing of the received functional acknowledgement so that e.g. a SAP IDOC SYSTAT message can be mapped from it and submitted to the connected SAP S/4HANA OnPremise system.

Pass Through

Does not support the simple handover of sender interchange message payloads.

It allows you to simply pass through sender interchanges to the receiver without changes in the payload itself, but with complete monitoring in the B2B Monitoring.

Sender EDI Interchange Custom Processing

EDI Splitter requires very specific content. Therefore, a slight modification of the header part in the sender interchange might be necessary. This is not supported yet.

It allows modifying the sender interchange payload in front of the EDI splitter via an additional custom integration flow.

Disable Sender EDI Interchange Syntax Validation

The syntax validation provided by the EDI Splitter is always enabled.

It allows to disable the syntax validation per custom parameter in a Business Transaction Activity.

B2B Monitoring - Business Document Split Event

The B2B Monitoring does not show specific entries for the interchange and per split message.

It enables to display one B2B Monitoring entry for the sender interchange payload and separate B2B Monitoring entries per split EDI message.

Sender XML Interchange Split Processing

Just splits EDI (ASC X12, UN/EDIFACT) sender interchange payloads, but not XML based type systems.

It also allows to split XML based type systems.

Set Receiver Interchange Control Numbers

It is not possible to individually set different control numbers for the interchange receiver payload and for the functional group inside of this interchange.

It allows to individually set different control numbers for the interchange and the functional group per Business Transaction Activity.

Customized Mapping Process

The current custom mapping process also requires the presence of syntactically correct source and target MIG. Custom mapping means a mapping, which is not created by the Integration Advisor.

It allows to enable custom mapping with just dummy MIGs, because the current release of the TPM requires the assignment of some source and target MIGs.

Returning Functional Ack. Interchange Modification

Just generates for sender interchanges with a functional acknowledgement required the predefined functional acknowledgement as supported by the EDI Splitter

It allows to define your own customizations and extensions in the already generated functional acknowledgement by the EDI Splitter.

Receiver Interchange Gather Bulk Processing

It does not gather split messages which belong together in an assembly of the receiver interchange.

It allows to gather messages that are split by the same transaction process so that these can be assembled for the receiver interchange.

Receiver Interchange Assembly Processing

It just enables the assembly and the insertion of just standard TPM configured content into the interchange/message header of the receiver interchange for the TPM supported type systems.

It allows to define own assembly flows with their own assembly rules and TPM configuration data insertion rules. This allows to set assembly flows for additional type systems.

XML to Other Syntax Conversion

It just provides the XML to EDI or vice versa conversion whereby the internal processing syntax is XML.

It allows to add further syntax conversions such as XML to CSV.

Toggle Sender- and Receiver-Name in Returning Functional Acknowledgement Interchange

The B2B Monitoring does not show the sender and receiver name in the correct places for generated and submitted functional acknowledgements. These must be toggled.

It toggles the sender and receiver names in generated functional acknowledgements so that these will be correctly shown in the B2B Monitoring.

Step 3 - Receiver Communication Flow - Extended → Integration Process: AS2 Receiver Channel and (SFTP) Receiver Channel

The AS2 receiver communication channel does not provide a possibility to set an expression for getting final names according to this expression.

It allows to set an expression for file names so that these will be dynamically generated via this expression. This expression can be set via custom parameter in each Business Transaction Activity.

Set Custom Search Attributes (with multiple values)

Just custom search attributes with one value can be set. For example, the business document numbers of several IDOC documents in one IDOC message can’t be collected.

It allows to set a list of values per search attribute.

Base-Overlay Approach

A Base-Overlay-Approach is not support. What is a Base.Overlay-Approach? Stay curious, because there will be a blog soon which explains the Base-Overlay Approach

It allows to enable the Base-Overlay-Approach.

 

How to get the B2B Integration Factory – Integration Packages?

If you use the TPM and you need one of the extensions above, we recommend to copy all B2B Integration Factory – Integration Packages into your own tenant. For this, there are two options:

  • Via the SAP Business Accelerator Hub in where you can discover and view this content.
  • Via Discovery tab in your own SAP Integration Suite tenant, from where you can copy the integration packages into your own workspace.

 

Via SAP Business Accelerator Hub

You can discover the B2B Integration Factory – Integration Packages in the SAP Business Accelerator Hub and add the integration packages into your tenant workspace via the following steps:

1.) Go to the SAP Business Accelerator Hub

  1. Go to More and
  2. Select Community
  3. Click View All Community at the bottom of this section

 

 

 


 

2.) If you see the whole list of community items

  1. Search for “B2B Integration Flows”
  2. Then you’ll just see the B2B Integration Factory - Integration Packages with the mentioned extensions.

 

 

3.) In each integration package tile

  1. You’ll find a button “Add to Workspace” for adding this content into your tenant workspace.
  2. This will open a new window “Add to workspace” in whereyou should agree to the terms of use of community content and click on “Confirm” button.Follow the instructions as described on the SAP Help page : Add a Package To a Workspace.
  3. Once the content is added to your workspace, follow the further installation and configuration instructions that you’ll find under Documents --> In the Configuration Guide - B2B Integration Factory - Trading Partner Management

 

 

Via your SAP Integration Suite Tenant

Alternatively, you can directly obtain the B2B Integration Packages in your SAP Integration Suite tenant via the following steps:

1.) You go in your SAP Integration Suite tenant to Discover à Integrations

  1. Search for “B2B Integration Factory”
  2. You will see all the mentioned integration packages in the overview list.

 

2.) Open each “B2B Integration Factory – xx” integration package

  1. Click on button “Copy” for copying it into your tenant workspace.
  2. Follow the further instructions that you’ll find under Documents --> in the Configuration Guide - B2B Integration Factory - Trading Partner Management

entry.title: Re: Kyma Integration with SAP Cloud Logging. Part 2: Let's ship some traces
entry.content:
Hi @alaajamal ,

that is a very good finding. Indeed, Istio/Envoy does not set the span status in case of a 5xx, instead it sets a custom attribute to the span "error=true", probably for historical reasons, see also the the documentation. In general Envoy is not following yet the OTel conventions, as depicted in this ticket.

Indeed the telemetry module does not support custom transformations yet, but it is very meaningful feature for the future, please follow this ticket to stay updated. Anyway, I think that can be one of the big features of the telemetry module, to fix such problems by convention, so I created a ticket for this, any feedback is welcome.

entry.title: Re: BTP Toolbox: App to Backup & Restore HDI Containers
entry.content:
Hi @Ben, 

many thanks for sharing your app with the community.
In fact, SAP has failed from the beginning to provide a solution for backup/restore of HDI containers with SAP HANA Cloud, which is especially important as multiple CAP applications typically share one HANA instance.
We also use HDI container export/import as fallback mechanism in case of complex changes. Up to now we do this manually. Automating the HDI container export/import would be a great improvement, of course.

I checked your README on GitHub and I am not sure I understood the architecture of your application correctly.   
We have several HANA instances (one for DEV, QAS and PRD). 
Could I use one instance of your application to backup/restore HDI containers of several HANA DBs?
Or would I need to deploy one instance of the application per HANA instance?
You write that the application could be used to copy an HDI container from PRD to DEV (for instance). Would this still be possible if PRD and DEV HDI container are deployed into two different HANA instances (as it is the case in our environment)?

Many thanks in advance and best regards, 
  Alex

Best regards,

Gernot

entry.title: S/4 HANA Public Cloud Edition / Recieve E-Invoices
entry.content:
Hallo Experts,

i have a question regarding the ability to recieve e-invoices (for example X-Rechnung here in Germany) in the Public Cloud.
So far we have implemented the sending of X-Rechnung via Email in our TDD System, but what if the company wants to recieve an invoice from a supplier who also sends it from his Public Cloud System via Email (in the X-Rechnung format).
What is the Best Practice for this Scenario ? How can we import this supplier invoice from the email and create it in the Public Cloud manually or even automatically ? 
SAP DRC doesnt seem to offer this funcitonality yet. Can we leverage Central Invoice Management or SAP Buisness Network ?
Thanks in advance for all suggestions.

Best regards
Lukas

entry.title: ABAP & XML
entry.content:

Then I need to do it reverse.  Read the XML responses, update the database field and report the updates.

I have over 25 years ABAP experience but haven't done anything with XML!!  If someone could just point me in the right direction to do minimal development but also using best practices, I'd greatly appreciate it!!

entry.title: Oracle 19c Database to S4 Table data migration using SAP DATA SERVICES
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

I am currently working on a migration project from an Oracle database to an S/4 HANA application system running on AWS. We have successfully set up datastore connections in SAP BODS for both the Oracle DB (datastore name: POFSN) and the S/4 HANA system (datastore name: SW1). Below is a brief overview of the connections:

S/4 HANA SYSTEM (SW1):

ORACLE Database (POFSN)

 

The issue arises when I attempt to drag a table (e.g., ADRC) from the SW1 datastore into the Dataflow in SAP BODS. I am not being given the option to set ADRC as the target table.

Is this process incorrect? What should I check to resolve this issue and ensure that tables from the S/4 HANA system can be used as target tables? 

Additionally, I would appreciate suggestions on other approaches to overwrite tables in S/4 HANA using SAP BODS.

Please let me know if any further information is required.

Thank you!

TAGS
SAP Data Services 
SAP S/4HANA 
Oracle Database

entry.title: Error CSRF Token Validation Failed in POST API
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am getting error "CSRF Token Validation Failed" in POST API. 

I am able to generate CSRF token successfully through below code. But seems token is not getting set hence getting the above error. 

Please suggest where I am doing wrong. Also, I am able to post successfully via POSTMAN with same data and Basic Authentication.

Many Thanks

DATA(lv_string) = cl_web_http_utility=>encode_base64( lv_Str ).

 

lv_string = |Basic { lv_string }|.

** Set up header fields for getting X_CSRF-TOKEN

DATA(lo_request2) = lo_http_client->get_http_request( ).

lo_request2->set_header_fields( VALUE #(

( name = 'Content-Type' value = 'application/json' )

( name = 'X-CSRF-Token' value = 'fetch' )

( name = 'Authorization' value = lv_string )

 

 

) ).

 

CONCATENATE i_url1 '/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_MAINTNOTIFICATION/MAINTENANCENOTIFICATION' INTO i_url2.

lo_request2->set_uri_path( i_uri_path = i_url2 ).

DATA(lv_response2) = lo_http_client->execute( i_method = if_web_http_client=>get )->get_header_fields( ).

READ TABLE lv_response2 INTO DATA(ls_repsonse2) WITH KEY name = 'x-csrf-token'.

IF sy-subrc = 0.

DATA(lv_token) = ls_repsonse2-value.

CLEAR ls_repsonse2.

ENDIF.

 

IF lv_token IS NOT INITIAL.

 

** Set CSRF Token

lo_http_client->set_csrf_token( ).

 

 

" Navigate to the resource and create a request for the create operation

lo_request = lo_client_proxy->create_resource_for_entity_set( 'MAINTENANCE_NOTIFICATION' )->create_request_for_create( ).

 

 

ENDIF.

entry.title: Error adding oAttachments2 to oPurchaseQuotations using B1 DI Server API
entry.content:
Hello All:

  I need to add oAttachment objects to oPurchaseQuotations. The physical file was uploaded previously in another process, ACT1 was not updated in this process. 

 I  executed this oAttachment2 XML file:

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-16" ?>
<env:Envelope xmlns:env="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/">
<env:Header>
<SessionID>5GA1fP7hfApH8vTWL1ukllN/OJOZVoU/dXcUGMxRlL4D1QsUBTq/Q6wdBtqVPsrs</SessionID>
</env:Header>
<env:Body>
<AddObject xmlns="http://127.0.0.1/SAP/">
<BOM>
<BO>
<AdmInfo>
<Object>oAttachments2</Object>
</AdmInfo>
<Attachments2_Lines>
<row>
<SourcePath>\\myURL\aFolder</SourcePath>
<FileName>fileName</FileName>
<FileExtension>pdf</FileExtension>
</row>
<row>
<SourcePath>\\myURL\aFolder</SourcePath>
<FileName>anotherFileName</FileName>
<FileExtension>pdf</FileExtension>
</row>
</Attachments2_Lines>
</BO>
</BOM>
</AddObject>
</env:Body>
</env:Envelope>

The response from DI server is:

<?xml version="1.0"?>
<env:Envelope xmlns:env="http://www.w3.org/2003/05/soap-envelope">
<env:Body>
<env:Fault>
<env:Code>
<env:Value>env:Receiver</env:Value>
<env:Subcode>
<env:Value>-10</env:Value>
</env:Subcode>
</env:Code>
<env:Reason>
<env:Text xml:lang="en">A file with this name already exists. [ATC1.FileName][line: 1] , '\\myURL\aFolder\fileName.pdf'</env:Text>
</env:Reason>
<env:Detail>
<Object>221</Object>
<ObjectIndex>1</ObjectIndex>
<Command>AddObject</Command>
<SessionID>77AD6292-9C3B-41ED-B34F-3A1BC35AC07A</SessionID>
</env:Detail>
</env:Fault>
</env:Body>
</env:Envelope>

 

Then I renamed the files to avoid the previous error and the response from DI Server is:

<?xml version="1.0"?>
<env:Envelope xmlns:env="http://www.w3.org/2003/05/soap-envelope">
<env:Body>
<env:Fault>
<env:Code>
<env:Value>env:Receiver</env:Value>
<env:Subcode>
<env:Value>-5002</env:Value>
</env:Subcode>
</env:Code>
<env:Reason>
<env:Text xml:lang="en">Source file does not exist , ''\\myURL\aFolder\fileName.pdf'</env:Text>
</env:Reason>
<env:Detail>
<Object>221</Object>
<ObjectIndex>1</ObjectIndex>
<Command>AddObject</Command>
<SessionID>F1609CD3-F5A5-485A-B39C-5F98B4532F81</SessionID>
</env:Detail>
</env:Fault>
</env:Body>
</env:Envelope>

What's the process to do this?

 

Best regards

entry.title: Re: RAP: Side Effect for child entity table
entry.content:
Hi @lars_kr,

Perhaps my answer is a little bit late but I've managed to do something similar in a different way, so perhaps I can share my experience with you.

In my scenario I have to prefill a Child Entity table with some entries whenever we create a draft instance. Keep in mind that this example only works with draft instances. 

The first step what I have done is to add an action that will prefill the data. This action is then represented as a button on the page. Here your approach is a little bit different, since you want to prefill it using when entering a field value.

 

  action fillChildTable;

 

 

After adding the action, I subscribe the action as a side effect on the child entity: 

 

  side effects { action fillChildTable affects entity _Child; }

 

In the implementation of the action to prefill the draft child entities I do the following: 

  1. Read the Child entity in Local Mode - to find out if it was prefilled already or not.
  2. Prefill the Child entity in draft mode.

The sample code looks something like that: 

 

  METHOD fillchildtable.
    READ ENTITIES OF entity IN LOCAL MODE
      ENTITY childentity
        ALL FIELDS WITH CORRESPONDING #( keys )
      RESULT DATA(lt_result)
      FAILED DATA(ls_failed)
      REPORTED DATA(ls_reported).

    IF lt_result IS INITIAL.

      DATA lt_table_for_create TYPE TABLE FOR CREATE entity\_childentity.

        APPEND VALUE #( %cid_ref = keys[ 1 ]-%cid_ref
                        %is_draft = keys[ 1 ]-%is_draft
                        %pid = keys[ 1 ]-%pid
                        %target = VALUE #( ( %cid = lv_cid
                                             %is_draft = keys[ 1 ]-%is_draft
                                             field = '123
                                             %pidparent = keys[ 1 ]-%pid
                                             %control-field = if_abap_behv=>mk-on ) ) ) TO lt_table_for_create.

      ENDLOOP.

      MODIFY ENTITIES OF entity IN LOCAL MODE
      ENTITY entity
         CREATE BY \_childentity FROM lt_table_for_create
      MAPPED DATA(lt_mapped).



    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.

 

Here you should take into consideration a few fields from the keys structure, e.g. %PID which in this case will be your Draft UUID, as well the %CID and the %IS_DRAFT field - since I am creating entries in a draft scenario, it is always draft ( '01' ). It is required to also include the %PID and %IS_DRAFT fields so the RAP Framework knows that you are creating draft entities in local mode.

I've tried implementing something similar with determination on a field and side effects to this determination, however my determination was not triggered when changing the Draft entity, therefore I opted for a Action instead. To be honest it looks cleaner to me with a button, and the users also seems to like it. Perhaps it should also be possible to implement a similar solution with a determination + side effects.

Keep in mind that this implementation is done with unmanaged scenario with draft.

entry.title: Re: Creating side-by-side process extensions from SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Teja,

Please note the side-by-side extensions are loosely coupled with SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud. What that means - the SAP S/4HANA processes by default do not wait for the side-by-side extension processes to complete the execution. They generally do not have transactional dependency. Maybe, the process in SAP BTP might be running assuming that the SAP S/4HANA business object is in a specific state, however the business object could have changed the state. You need to handle such lifecycle object control explicitly by listening to multiple lifecycle events, usage of attributes to maintain state, etc. If you can do that in SAP BTP or your use case for process extension does not require such transactional consistency then you can implement a side-by-side process extension.

entry.title: Re: Kyma Integration with SAP Cloud Logging. Part 2: Let's ship some traces
entry.content:
As mentioned in the envoy docs, the attribute will be enriched only for 5xx. I tested that in combination with Cloud Logging and the attribute is present. If now 4xx should be treated different by Envoy is really hard to judge, following for example the discussion here.

As mentioned, we will see to add the automatic conversion short-term and in mid-term we will add support for custom transformations.

entry.title: How to Enable SolMan Incident Approval Directly from Email in SAP Solution Manager?
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

In SAP Solution Manager, when a user creates a ticket, an email notification is automatically sent to the department manager for approval. Currently, the department manager needs to log into SAP Solution Manager to change the ticket status from 'Open' to 'Release.'

I want to enhance this process so that the department manager can approve the ticket directly from the email notification itself. Specifically, when the manager clicks 'Approve' in the email, the ticket status in SAP Solution Manager should automatically update from 'Open' to 'Release.'

My questions are:

  1. How can I integrate this functionality with the email triggered by SAP Solution Manager?

  2. Is it feasible to implement this kind of email-based approval within SAP Solution Manager?

Any guidance or suggestions would be greatly appreciated!

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Modify Form Route Map pertaining to PMGM

entry.title: Re: Plant Maintenance module without HR(SAP HCM)
entry.content:
Hello KerryOnn,
So yeah, Markus already gave you the answer.
On the other hand, a small thing from my side.

In PM/EAM what we essentially need is so called Mini HR.
Yes, it's still HR Module, but it doesn't mean it has to be fully configured and reflect the reality.
If your company does not use it, has some 3rd party solution for it etc. - just do the dummy config, only for PM/EAM purpose.
It will make your life easier. 

Especially when u run S/4HANA. 
You create Personnel Numbers, do the BP replication and that's it.
And you are can leverage for instance RSH and other things. 

So yeah, think about that as well.

Cheers,
Dan

entry.title: Emarsys reporting is not showing impressions for Mobile
entry.content:
I have a web channel campaign that is set to be shown in mobile too, but after some tests, I've seen that this campaign is not even performing on mobile. The impressions show 0 for all KPIs under Mobile.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Emarsys reporting is not showing impressions for Mobile
entry.content:
This is currently a system limitation as Web Channel reporting cannot differentiate between desktop and mobile impressions in Visual Editor-based campaigns.

To check the impressions for each platform - desktop and mobile - you must design the campaigns using the HTML editor. This is explained at SAP Emarsys Help: Editor types

entry.title: MB90 It does not replicate on the first attempt IDOC MBGMCR03
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have a problem, when executing transaction MB90, I notice that, if you stay on the next screen after the execution, the IDoc always remains in state 30.

IDoc in status 30:

I have noticed that to resolve this issue, it is necessary to remain on the initial screen of transaction MB90

And the IDoc changes its status to successful.

My question is: Is this the standard behavior of the transaction?

Note: I have stayed on this screen for more than 20 minutes, and the IDoc has always been in status 30, but when I went back, the status changed. I have tried the scenario several times, and it only works when I go back.

I also have a background job running, but it only processes the IDoc when I go back to the initial MB90 screen

Could you please help me determine if this is standard behavior, or what it might be due to? Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: Unrestricted stock in storage locations for maintenance orders using fiori apps
entry.content:
Hello David,
If you are talking about Spare Parts Consumption (movement 261) check out the Fiori App 'Perform Maintenance Jobs'.
Once work order is planned, spare parts are assigned - you can book consumption there, and if there is no stock - u got an error straight in the app.

However, if you mean, that you want to see the unrestricted stock during planning, during spare parts assignment - most probably u need a small development.
On the other side it's a bit tricky. 
It's not about planning work and using spare parts which are on the stock.
It's about assigning the right spare parts, and if there is no stock - buying them.

Have a look at the app and let me know what u think.
Cheers,
Dan

entry.title: Driving Business Process Transformation through Innovation
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

We are thrilled to introduce SAP Signavio Lab Space, an innovative cloud-based solution designed to empower businesses to stay ahead of the curve in process transformation. This dedicated platform offers companies the opportunity to discover and experiment with SAP Signavio's ongoing innovation initiatives in a risk-free environment.

What's in it for the Customer?

SAP Signavio Lab Space is accessible to new and existing customers of Process Manager and offers accelerated access to beta features. By exploring ongoing innovation projects within SAP Signavio Lab Space, customers can gain insights into the latest developments and easily upload and test their own data in a risk-free environment, empowering users to sample and shape future innovations.

Why SAP Signavio Lab Space?

Here are the key reasons why businesses should leverage SAP Signavio Lab Space to propel their process management initiatives forward:

  1. Discover Ongoing Innovation Projects: Unlock the potential of a centralized platform that provides updates and insight into ongoing innovation projects. Access a repository of emerging concepts to stay ahead of the curve in a risk-free environment for experimentation.
  2. Validate Innovations with Your Own Data: Upload and test your own datasets to witness firsthand how your data can drive innovation and improvement within your specific business context.
  3. Help Shape Our Roadmap: Execute and manage improvement initiatives efficiently and quantify the delivered value of improvement initiatives. 

How to Access SAP Signavio Lab Space?

Users with an SAP Signavio Enterprise or Enterprise Plus license can access SAP Signavio Lab Space. To get limited free access to beta features, interested parties can contact us at signavio_lab_space@sap.com.

Check out the overview video for SAP Signavio Lab Space here!
